problem_statement: The fixed bottom navigation currently carries five labels plus the theme toggle, which competes with the app's real tasks on small screens. Shell spacing is also static, so some views feel cramped on phone while tablet layouts still underuse the available width.
in_scope: Remove the theme toggle from bottom navigation and surface appearance controls only in Settings
Keep the current five destinations while making Scan the visually primary navigation action
Retune shell spacing and bottom padding so content clears the nav and safe-area consistently on phone and tablet
requirements: Bottom navigation keeps Home Pantry Scan Plan Settings in the current order
Theme switching is no longer rendered inside the bottom navigation
Shell padding accounts for nav height and safe-area so primary controls do not sit behind the nav
acceptance_criteria: Phone views no longer show the theme toggle in the bottom nav
Scan is the most visually emphasized nav item without changing route structure
Core screens remain fully visible and tappable above the bottom nav on iPhone and iPad viewport sizes
risks: Tablet spacing may feel too sparse if shell width and padding are not tuned together
implementation_notes: Update src/app/(app)/layout.tsx src/components/BottomNav.tsx and shell spacing rules in src/app/globals.css. Keep the product visually quiet rather than adding extra iconography or badges.
